Item: Flight Controller Set (FC + GPS + compass + power)
Part / Model: Holybro Pixhawk 6C Standard Set (Pixhawk 6C + PM06 14S power module + M10 GPS/compass + cable set)
Where to Buy (link): https://holybro.com/products/pixhawk-6c  (Standard Set / M10 GPS)  |  AliExpress: 'Holybro Pixhawk 6C M10 GPS set'
Notes: PM06 power module + M10 GPS/compass INCLUDED. VERIFIED $199 (Holybro/AliExpress); US retail (pyrodrone) $245.99 and OUT OF STOCK. The 6C is exactly the FC Holybro pairs with the X500 V2 dev platform - validates it as the right controller for this build. Uses analog PM06 (PM02D digital NOT compatible).

Part / Model: Carbon-fiber 500mm quadcopter frame, X500 V2 style (full CF, Raspberry Pi / Pixhawk platform mounts)
Where to Buy (link): AliExpress: search '500mm carbon fiber quadcopter frame X500'  (true-CF clone)  |  reference: genuine Holybro X500 V2 Frame Kit = $175.49 at getfpv (over budget)
Notes: PLA is NOT airworthy at ~1.5kg (softens ~60C, brittle under vibration/impact) - this CF frame removes that risk. PRICE IS AN ESTIMATE (~$50-80, varies by listing - not pinned like the verified lines). BEFORE ORDERING verify: (1) motor mount fits the D2830 bolt pattern (X500 = 16x16mm; use a ~$3 adapter if needed); (2) 500mm clears 11in props (75mm tip gap - OK) but frame is rated 10in, so 10in/1045 props are the in-spec alternative. 4-in-1 ESC mounts on the center plate, motor wires routed through the hollow arms.

Part / Model: Holybro SiK Telemetry Radio V3, 100mW 433MHz (air + ground set)
Notes: VERIFIED $58.99 (Holybro). Independent GCS link for live mission monitoring + abort if the Pi/WiFi drops - the key safety upgrade for autonomous flight. Plug-and-play with QGroundControl; >300m stock, km-class with a ground patch antenna. Connect to FC TELEM1. 433MHz for the region (915MHz = Americas) - verify local IMDA allocation.

VERIFIED ~$55-65. Single pack gives ~18 min hover at this AUW. A 2nd pack is a low-cost post-build add for back-to-back field sessions. 50-70C rating = ~5x current headroom over the 58A full-throttle peak. XT60 matches the PM06.

Fresh balanced blades for clean position hold. Owned 11in props can be REUSED instead ($0) - if the frame lands above ~$75, drop this line and reuse them to hold the $400 line. If running 10in props to match the frame spec, buy 1045 instead.
Within the $400 budget (~$15 margin). The frame is the only estimated price; the rest are verified.
Verified 875g pull each = 3,500g total -> ~2.3:1 thrust-to-weight at ~1.5kg AUW (>=2:1 = good for an autonomous platform). Max 14.5A draw each.
60A/channel vs 14.5A draw = 4x headroom. Mounts on the frame center plate; motor wires route through the carbon arms. Retained over individual ESCs+PDB (lighter, simpler, fewer failure points).

3-wire UART (TX/RX/GND) to the FC; bridges MAVLink to GCS over WiFi while the SiK radio provides the independent link. CSI camera port non-functional; no camera in this build. Mounts on the frame platform board (designed for Pi 4).
REQUIRED but handled outside this grant. Do NOT power the Pi from the FC BEC. Size for Pi 4B (+ any USB load) at 5V/3A minimum.

~$1, NON-NEGOTIABLE on a 4S system before first power-on. Solder across the ESC battery pads to absorb inductive voltage spikes that otherwise destroy the ESC. (Trivial cost; listed for completeness.)

Assumed already owned. Needed for manual override during bring-up and first flights - do not fly a maiden without a manual kill option even on an autonomous build. If NOT owned, this is a separate budget item.

Order-ready. Validated: AUW ~1.5kg, thrust-to-weight ~2.3:1, hover ~18 min, ESC 4x headroom, battery 5x headroom, 11in props clear the 500mm frame. PLA frame replaced with CF (airworthiness fix). Open pre-order checks: motor-mount bolt pattern + prop size on the chosen frame.
